perturbation.original_text: 1.1.3 “Buyer ” – means the person/entity described and identified as such i n the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement;
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1.3 “Buyer ” – means the person/entity described and identified as such i n the Prospect’s Profile on the face of this Agreement;
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Buyer's Profile" to "Prospect's Profile" introduces ambiguity as the document consistently refers to "Buyer" elsewhere. This creates confusion on whether the obligations apply to all prospects or only confirmed buyers, and now the Buyer's Profile is a reference to something else, as the document does not have a definition for "Prospect".
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Buyer record s that it is (and/ or has been and/or will be) partaking in investigations of (and/or negotiations with) various Seller/s and/or Business/es , with a view towards potentially acquiring such Business/es. The Buyer further acknowledges that it is serious in its investigations towards potentially acquiring a Business.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Buyer's Profile" to "Prospect's Profile" in the definition of "Buyer" introduces a contradiction. The contradicted text (2.1) explicitly refers to 'The Buyer' and their actions. If 'Buyer' is now defined in relation to 'Prospect's Profile', it creates ambiguity whether the obligations outlined in 2.1 apply to all prospects or only confirmed buyers. This contradicts the clear expectation that 'Buyer' refers to a defined entity with specific obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 1.1.6 “Seller/s ” – means each of the owners, of each of the Business/es introduced by Aldes to the Buyer from time to time, independently and separately .
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1.6 “Seller/s ” – means each of the owners, of each of the Business/es introduced by Aldes to the Customer from time to time, independently and separately .
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Buyer" to "Customer" in the definition of "Seller/s" introduces ambiguity. The agreement relies on a consistent understanding of who the "Buyer" is. By changing the definition, it is now unclear to whom Aldes is introducing the sellers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 The Buyer acknowledges that Aldes will be deemed and regarded (and will be) the effective cause of any transaction which may transpire and/or materialise between the Buyer and any Business/es and/or Seller/s .
perturbation.justification: The change from "Buyer" to "Customer" in the definition of "Seller/s" directly contradicts section 6.1. Section 6.1 explicitly refers to 'the Buyer' acknowledging Aldes' role. By changing the defined term to 'Customer', the agreement becomes unclear on who is acknowledging Aldes' role in the transaction as the term Buyer is used in the contradicted text, creating a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the change causes confusion by altering a previously defined term.

perturbation.original_text: 4.  UNDERTAKING OF CONFIDENTIALITY 4.1 The Buyer undertakes , towards and in favour of each of Aldes, the Business/es and the Seller/s (jointly and severally) , with regard to and in respect of any and all of the Confidential Information and/or Confidential Materials, that it will keep same (irrespective of how same was disclosed to the Buyer ) strictly confidential and secret and that it will hold same in absolute trust and confidence . _______________ Initial BUYER ’S CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT
perturbation.changed_text: 4.  UNDERTAKING OF CONFIDENTIALITY 4.1 The Party undertakes , towards and in favour of each of Aldes, the Business/es and the Seller/s (jointly and severally) , with regard to and in respect of any and all of the Confidential Information and/or Confidential Materials, that it will keep same (irrespective of how same was disclosed to the Party ) strictly confidential and secret and that it will hold same in absolute trust and confidence . _______________ Initial BUYER ’S CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT
perturbation.explanation: Changing Buyer to "The Party" creates confusion in this entire section. There is no definition as to what party is this referring to. The title of the document itself still refers to "BUYER". This also changes the understanding of who is subject to the confidentiality undertaking.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 The Buyer acknowledges that it will be liable , responsible and accountable to each of Aldes , the Business/es and the Seller/s (jointly and severally) for a breach of this Agreement by itself or any of its related or inter related parties, employees, consultants, ad visers and/or agents.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines 'Buyer' as the party undertaking confidentiality. The changed text replaces 'Buyer' with 'The Party', but the document title and other sections, including 5.1, still refer to 'Buyer'. Section 5.1 explicitly states the 'Buyer' is liable for breaches. By changing 'Buyer' to 'The Party' in section 4.1, but keeping section 5.1 as is, creates an ambiguity as to who is liable for a breach of confidentiality, since 'The Party' is not clearly defined and may not be equivalent to the 'Buyer'. This constitutes a contradiction because the agreement's enforceability is now in question regarding who the confidentiality obligations apply to.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 The Buyer acknowledges that it will be liable , responsible and accountable to each of Aldes , the Business/es and the Seller/s (jointly and severally) for a breach of this Agreement by itself or any of its related or inter related parties, employees, consultants, ad visers and/or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 The Acquiring Party acknowledges that it will be liable , responsible and accountable to each of Aldes , the Business/es and the Seller/s (jointly and severally) for a breach of this Agreement by itself or any of its related or inter related parties, employees, consultants, ad visers and/or agents.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Buyer" with "Acquiring Party" without defining "Acquiring Party" introduces uncertainty as the agreement defines 'Buyer'. Liability becomes unclear because the document doesn't specify who 'Acquiring Party' refers to, or their relationship to the Buyer. Therefore, the 'Acquiring Party' might have different obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 The Buyer notes that Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. As such, the Buyer warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly identifies 'Buyer' as the party responsible for certain obligations. The changed text replaces 'Buyer' with 'Acquiring Party' without defining 'Acquiring Party'. Clause 8.1 refers specifically to the 'Buyer' and the information they presented in their 'Buyer's Profile'. By changing 'Buyer' to 'Acquiring Party' in clause 5.1, it creates uncertainty as to whether the 'Acquiring Party' is also bound by the obligations outlined in clause 8.1 related to the 'Buyer's Profile'. This introduces a contradiction, as the agreement now uses a different term to refer to the party undertaking the obligations, thus conflicting with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2 The Buyer warrants and guarantees that it will never circumvent Aldes (albeit directly itself or indirectly through any of its subsidiaries, holding companies, group companies, related parties and/or inter -related parties or otherwise indirectly ), in negot iating or concluding any transactions , arrangements and/or agreements contemplated in clauses 2.1 and/or 2.2 above with (or with regard to) any Seller/s and/or Business /es. For avoidance of doubt, the Buyer will at all times act in good faith and will only transact and deal with Seller/s and/or Business/es, via the brokerage , intervention and mediation of Aldes.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 The Client warrants and guarantees that it will never circumvent Aldes (albeit directly itself or indirectly through any of its subsidiaries, holding companies, group companies, related parties and/or inter -related parties or otherwise indirectly ), in negot iating or concluding any transactions , arrangements and/or agreements contemplated in clauses 2.1 and/or 2.2 above with (or with regard to) any Seller/s and/or Business /es. For avoidance of doubt, the Client will at all times act in good faith and will only transact and deal with Seller/s and/or Business/es, via the brokerage , intervention and mediation of Aldes.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Buyer" to "Client" creates a contradiction, especially considering "Buyer" is previously defined in the document and "Client" is not. It introduces uncertainty as to whether all responsibilities are transferrable as the document continuously refers to "Buyer".
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1.3 “Buyer ” – means the person/entity described and identified as such i n the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement;
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Buyer', which is defined in section 1.1.3 as the person/entity described in the Buyer's Profile. The changed text replaces 'Buyer' with 'Client'. Because 'Client' is not defined anywhere, this change creates a contradiction because the document continuously refers to 'Buyer', and now the responsibilities are not clearly attributed, causing a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 The Buyer notes that Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. As such, the Buyer warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 The Interested Party notes that Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. As such, the Interested Party warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.
perturbation.explanation: Using "Interested Party" instead of "Buyer" in section 8.1 creates ambiguity because section 8.1 outlines obligations and representations based on the Buyer's profile. By referring to "Interested Party," the text implies that someone other than the defined Buyer is bound by the warranties related to the Buyer's Profile, which is contradictory and confusing. This conflict in which entity warrants all information of the "Buyer's profile", makes it unclear who is bound to the accuracy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 The Interested Party notes that Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. As such, the Interested Party warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Buyer' with 'Interested Party' in section 8.1, which outlines obligations based on the 'Buyer's Profile'. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, highlights that the 'Interested Party' now warrants the accuracy of the 'Buyer's Profile'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear if the 'Interested Party' is the same as the 'Buyer' or a separate entity, leading to ambiguity about who is responsible for the Buyer's Profile information, as stated in the perturbation explanation, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, thus a strong contradiction exists.
